mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate directly raises the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a sudden labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, causing institutional investors to de-risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in the residential demand cycle, which boosts financial sector earnings and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or walk-forward Sharpe ratio of the trading strategy falls below 0.5 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that reduces input cost pressures and improves corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in backtests spanning 2015-2022.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that forces central banks to initiate aggressive tightening, triggering equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets price in tightening ahead of the trigger, resulting in positive forward returns in >50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue growth, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months with payroll growth, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0 percent signals a severe demand contraction that forces earnings downgrades across cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: Earnings revisions remain positive despite low GDP prints, or forward returns show positive direction in >55% of low-growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling consumer price inflation indicates supply chain normalization that alleviates cost-push pressures, allowing central banks to pause tightening and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence fails to predict positive returns when inflation remains above 5%, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.
